subject: Project Management
title: Pair Programming
description: A quick, practical guide to Pair Programming.

hook.text: Two developers, one computer. One types (the 'driver'), the other thinks (the 'navigator').

buildup.text: Kent Beck made pair programming a core XP practice. Managers thought it halved productivity.

discovery.text: Studies showed pairs produced fewer bugs and more maintainable code — total cost often dropped.

twist.text: The real benefit wasn't code quality — it was knowledge sharing. No single point of failure.

climax.text: Pair programming proved that two heads aren't just better — they're cheaper in the long run.

punchline.text: It looks like waste. It's actually an investment.

quiz.question: What was the surprising benefit of pair programming?
options: Knowledge sharing eliminated single points of failure
It eliminated the need for code reviews
